preview_text:
The model rates Mexico as a heavy favourite at 83%, leaving South Africa just 1% to win. The sportsbook consensus is considerably more cautious, placing Mexico at 67%—a gap of 16 percentage points. The Polymarket contract shows no conviction, pricing all three outcomes equally at 33% each, likely reflecting thin liquidity or hedging rather than genuine uncertainty.

The model's distribution reflects a match expected to be largely one-directional. Draws are possible at 16%, but the overwhelming bulk of probability accrues to a Mexico victory. The sportsbook agrees on the likely winner but redistributes significantly toward both draw and South Africa upset scenarios, suggesting it sees plausible paths to either outcome that the model dismisses.

The divergence between model and market is substantial and material. The model's 83% Mexico win probability stands 16 points above the sportsbook median, while the sportsbook allows South Africa 11% rather than 1%. This dashboard does not adjudicate which estimate is more reliable. The Polymarket prices appear uninformative and are noted for completeness only.
